text: It is necessary to secure the forced ventilation to a compartment where there is a fire to:

options.text: Prevent additional oxygen from reaching the fire
Retain toxic gases to help smother flames
Protect the ventilation fan motor from overspeeding
Limit the expansion of cooling steam lines

explanation: Forcing ventilation into a compartment with a fire provides an additional supply of oxygen, which fuels the combustion process. Securing the ventilation (Option A) removes this oxygen source, aiding in fire suppression, consistent with removing an element of the fire triangle.
